title: Novae Linguae Model Weights (v0.1)
description: A content-addressed POINTER record for model weights (spec/weights.md): identity (base model + per-file sha256 blob manifest), provenance (the deterministic training recipe over content-addressed corpus data), and advisory fetch locations. The blobs themselves are NOT stored in the commons — they live on ordinary static hosting and are verified client-side against files[].sha256 (the host is untrusted; the hash is the truth). Hashed like a function record (remove 'hash' before JCS-canonicalizing and BLAKE3-256 hashing), address prefix `wgt_`. Unsigned — provenance and measured capability are carried by signed eval-attestation records about this address, never by the pointer itself.

kind.description: Discriminates this artifact as a weights pointer record.

hash.description: BLAKE3-256 of the canonical serialization of this record with 'hash' removed.

name_hints.description: Non-normative human/agent-friendly names, as in function records.

model.description: The base model the weights apply to, e.g. 'Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct'.

license.description: The base model's license (rides along, not enforced — the record cannot launder it).

format.description: What the bytes are. Closed enum — adding a format requires a schema version bump.

files.description: The blob manifest. sha256 (not BLAKE3) deliberately: blobs are not JCS-canonicalizable JSON, so any collision-resistant hash serves, and sha256 is the ML-ecosystem convention (safetensors, HF).

name.description: File name within the adapter directory, e.g. 'adapter_model.safetensors'.

recipe.description: The deterministic derivation: same base + recipe reproduces files[].sha256 bit-for-bit. This is the reproducibility claim a certifier can re-execute.

description.description: How the corpus was produced, e.g. 'gen_corpus.py --combinatorial (corpus12, 42 template families)'.
How the split was produced, e.g. 'export_finetune.py --conventions off --shots 0 --holdout-corpus' (the leakage guard).

hyperparameters.description: Remaining knobs needed for bit-identical reproduction (lora rank/alpha/dropout, max-seq-len, lr, dtype, …).

measured.description: OPTIONAL self-reported eval summary. Normatively UNTRUSTED — consumers rely on signed eval-attestation records about this wgt_ address, never on this field.

urls.description: Advisory fetch locations, primary first (convention: <origin>/blobs/<sha256>). Any mirror may serve; files[].sha256 decides.

derived_from.description: Optional lineage pointer (e.g. the wgt_ address these weights were further trained from).

supersedes.description: The wgt_ address this record supersedes. Versioning is a supersedes chain, not mutable tags.
